findings.claim: The retained canonical learning must be reviewable as unique and accurate.
findings.observed: The task-scoped diff names learnings/tooling/verify-deleted-tracked-files-with-worktree-state.md but supplies no content hunk or separate artifact containing its text; the checkpoint only asserts what the learning explains.
findings.rationale: Without the retained learning content, semantic review cannot verify the explicit uniqueness and accuracy requirement from caller-supplied material.
findings.required: Supply the content-bearing diff or artifact for the retained learning so its uniqueness, accuracy, and canonical placement can be evaluated.

findings.claim: The explicit acceptance requirement that existing relevant tests pass needs canonical Test Gate evidence.
findings.observed: The caller-supplied canonical Test Gate status is not run and states that no Test Gate evidence exists; checkpoint prose claims verification but is not the canonical Test Gate reference.
findings.rationale: Acceptance cannot establish the test-pass portion of goal-005 from task-local prose when the caller-owned canonical Test Gate has supplied no result.
findings.required: Provide a completed canonical Test Gate reference establishing the relevant test outcome for this run.

goals.summary: Disposable `tmp/` content is ignored according to existing repository conventions.
Maintained code and documentation have no dependency on removed files.
Any retained learning is unique, accurate, and located under the existing canonical `learnings/` hierarchy.
Existing relevant tests pass and final diff contains no unrelated cleanup.
